code.text: CT Angiogram Head and Neck With Contrast

conclusion:
The left mid to upper cervical ICA is progressively narrowed in caliber with minimal opacification seen throughout its superior course as well as minimal opacification/occlusion of the intracranial left ICA.

No other CTA evidence of a large vessel occlusion or high-grade stenosis within the head or neck.

The visualized lung apices demonstrate groundglass and nodular opacities within the lungs bilaterally, right greater than left. This appears increased when compared to the prior CT chest from 7/9/2025. Consider a dedicated CT chest study for further evaluation.

Again seen is a pathologic compression deformity at C7 and additional lytic osseous metastatic disease involving the visualized spine.

Action required. +
